title: Phase 3: Tutorial Mode, New Enemy Types, and Progressive Levels

requirements.summary: Add a step-by-step Tutorial Mode with on-screen prompts guiding players through movement, throttle, shooting, lock-on, dodging missiles, and deploying flares, advancing only after successful completion of each step
acceptanceCriteria: Tutorial mode is accessible from the main menu or game start screen
Steps cover: movement, throttle, shooting, lock-on targeting, missile dodge, and flare deployment
Each step shows a clear on-screen instruction prompt
The tutorial only advances to the next step after the player successfully performs the required action
A progress indicator shows which tutorial step the player is on
Tutorial can be exited/skipped at any point
Completing the tutorial transitions the player to the first progressive level

file_operations.description: Add tutorial mode state management including tutorialStep, tutorialActive, tutorialCompleted flags and step validation logic. Track player actions (movement, throttle changes, shooting, lock-on, missile dodge, flare deployment) to detect successful completion of each tutorial step and auto-advance to the next. Add a tutorialStepData array defining each step's prompt text, completion condition, and target mechanic. Persist tutorialCompleted flag to localStorage.

Create a tutorial overlay component that displays the current step's instruction prompt, a progress indicator (e.g., 'Step 2 of 7'), and skip/exit buttons. Position the overlay at the top-center of the screen with semi-transparent backdrop. Accept props for tutorialStep, totalSteps, stepPrompt, onSkip, and onExit. Style consistently with existing F19HUD component using neon arcade theming.

Wire the F19TutorialOverlay component into the game page, rendering it conditionally when tutorialActive is true from useF19FlightSimulatorGame. Pass current tutorial step data and handlers for skip/exit actions. Ensure the tutorial overlay appears above the HUD but below the game-over and coin shop overlays in z-index stacking order.

requirements.summary: Implement four new enemy types with distinct behaviors and visuals: Fast Jets (high-speed, hard to track), Armored Enemies (multi-hit with health bar), Missile-Launching Drones (fire homing missiles), and Ground-to-Air Turrets (static, rapid-fire)
acceptanceCriteria: Fast Jets spawn and move at significantly higher speed than standard enemies and are visually distinct
Armored enemies display a health bar and require at least 3 hits to destroy
Missile-launching drones fire homing missiles that can be countered by deploying flares
Ground-to-air turrets are placed on terrain and fire projectiles upward at the player when within range
Each enemy type has a unique 3D model/geometry distinguishable from others
Each enemy type awards a different coin and score value on destruction
All new enemy types integrate with the existing targeting/lock-on system in useF19FlightSimulatorGame.ts

file_operations.description: Extend the enemy type system to support four new enemy variants: 'fastJet', 'armoredEnemy', 'missileDrone', and 'groundTurret'. Add enemy type-specific properties including speed multipliers, health values, missile-firing capability flags, and coin/score reward values. Implement logic for armored enemies to track current HP and only destroy when HP reaches zero. Add missile-launching behavior for drones that spawn homing missiles targeting the player at intervals. Add ground turret logic for static position enemies that fire rapid projectiles when player is in range and at low altitude. Ensure all new enemy types work with existing lock-on and targeting systems.

Add distinct 3D geometries for each new enemy type: Fast Jets (sleek elongated cone with swept wings), Armored Enemies (bulky box-based geometry with additional plating), Missile-Launching Drones (quad-rotor propeller design with missile pods), Ground-to-Air Turrets (cylindrical base with rotating gun barrel). Render health bars above armored enemies showing current HP out of max HP. Use different material colors/emissive properties to make each enemy type visually distinguishable at a distance.

requirements.summary: Add a Progressive Levels system with at least 6 levels of escalating difficulty, each combining multiple win conditions (survive time, destroy enemies, score threshold, protect objective) with increasing enemy count, speed, missile frequency, and new enemy types
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 progressive levels are defined with increasing difficulty parameters
Each level specifies a combination of at least 2 win conditions from: survive X seconds, destroy X enemies, reach score threshold, protect objective
Level objectives are displayed in an overlay at the start of each level
HUD shows live progress toward all active objectives during gameplay
Enemies become more numerous, faster, and include new enemy types in higher levels
A protect-the-objective mechanic exists where a target (e.g., carrier) has HP and the level fails if it is destroyed
A level-complete screen shows score, coins earned, and objectives met, with a button to proceed to the next level
A level-fail screen appears when the player crashes or fails to meet a time-limited objective, with a retry button
Levels are unlocked sequentially; completing a level unlocks the next

file_operations.description: Add progressive levels state management with a levelDefinitions array containing at least 6 levels, each specifying: level name, difficulty rating, combined win conditions (survive time, destroy enemy count, score threshold, protect objective HP), enemy spawn parameters (count, types, speed multipliers, missile frequency). Track currentLevel, levelProgress for each objective type, objectiveHP for protect missions, and levelUnlocked status array in localStorage. Implement logic to check all active win conditions each frame and trigger level completion or failure. Add levelComplete and levelFailed flags to control overlay display. Increment level unlock status on successful completion.

Create a level start overlay component that displays the current level's name, difficulty rating, and all active objectives with their target values (e.g., 'Survive 90 seconds', 'Destroy 15 enemies', 'Protect the carrier'). Include a 'Start Level' button to begin gameplay. Style with neon arcade theming consistent with F19HUD and tutorial overlay.

Create a level complete overlay showing 'Mission Complete' title, final score, coins earned this level, checkmarks for each completed objective, and a 'Next Level' button. Style with success-themed neon green accents and arcade font styling.

Create a level failed overlay showing 'Mission Failed' title, reason for failure (crashed, time expired, objective destroyed), and a 'Retry Level' button. Style with failure-themed neon red accents and arcade font styling.

Add an objectives progress panel to the HUD displaying live progress toward each active level objective: timer countdown for survive missions, enemy kill count vs target, current score vs threshold, and objective HP bar for protect missions. Position the objectives panel at the top-right of the HUD. Only display when in progressive levels mode (not freeplay or tutorial).

Wire the F19LevelStartOverlay, F19LevelCompleteOverlay, and F19LevelFailedOverlay components into the game page, rendering each conditionally based on level state flags from useF19FlightSimulatorGame. Handle transitions between overlays and gameplay states. Ensure level overlays have higher z-index than HUD but coexist with tutorial and shop overlays appropriately.

Add a health bar rendering above the aircraft carrier when in protect-objective mode, showing current HP out of max HP. Position the health bar above the island superstructure. Use neon styling consistent with enemy health bars.

requirements.summary: Add a Level Select / Mode Select screen showing Tutorial Mode, Progressive Levels (with lock/unlock status and best scores), and Freeplay option before starting gameplay
acceptanceCriteria: A mode/level select screen is shown when starting the F-19 game instead of going directly into freeplay
Tutorial Mode option is always unlocked and accessible
Progressive levels are listed with name, difficulty indicator, and locked/unlocked status
Locked levels show a lock icon; unlocked levels are clickable
Completed levels display the player's best score beneath their entry
A 'Freeplay' option to play the existing sandbox mode is also available

file_operations.description: Create a mode/level select screen component with three sections: Tutorial Mode (always unlocked with a 'Start Tutorial' button), Progressive Levels (a scrollable grid of level cards showing level name, difficulty stars, lock icon for locked levels, best score for completed levels, and click handler for unlocked levels), and Freeplay Mode (a card with 'Play Freeplay' button). Accept props for levelUnlockStatus array, levelBestScores map, onSelectTutorial, onSelectLevel, and onSelectFreeplay handlers. Style with neon arcade theming and use layout similar to the GameLobby grid.

Add modeSelectActive state flag that starts as true and is set to false when a mode is selected. Add selectedMode ('tutorial', 'level', 'freeplay') and selectedLevelIndex state. Load levelUnlockStatus and levelBestScores from localStorage on initialization. Export handlers for mode selection that update state and hide the mode select screen.

Wire the F19ModeSelect component to render when modeSelectActive is true from useF19FlightSimulatorGame, displaying it as a full-screen overlay that hides the 3D canvas and HUD until a mode is selected. Pass level unlock status, best scores, and mode selection handlers. Ensure the Canvas and game UI only render when modeSelectActive is false.
